entries.preparationInstructions: Remove metallic objects, jewelry, and undergarments with hooks from chest area. No other preparation needed.
entries.positioningInstructions: Patient standing upright, chest against the image receptor, arms rotated forward. Deep inspiration required.
contraindications: Pregnancy (relative; use lead shielding if essential)
entries.equipmentRequirements: Digital X-ray system or computed radiography (CR) system

entries.preparationInstructions: Remove metallic objects from chest area. Used for bedside/portable studies when patient cannot stand.
entries.positioningInstructions: Patient sitting or supine with image receptor behind the back. X-ray tube in front.
contraindications: Pregnancy (relative; use lead shielding if essential)
entries.facilityRequirements: Portable X-ray unit or radiology room
entries.equipmentRequirements: Portable or fixed digital X-ray system

entries.preparationInstructions: Remove metallic objects from abdominal area. No special preparation required for acute abdomen studies.
entries.positioningInstructions: Erect: Patient standing. Supine: Patient lying flat. Both views typically obtained for acute abdomen.
contraindications: Pregnancy (relative; use lead shielding if essential)

entries.preparationInstructions: Remove rings, bracelets, and other jewelry from the hand and wrist.
entries.positioningInstructions: Hand placed flat on cassette (PA view) and 45-degree oblique. Fingers spread apart.
contraindications: Pregnancy (relative; use lead shielding for abdomen)
entries.equipmentRequirements: Digital X-ray system with extremity positioning aids

entries.preparationInstructions: Remove metallic objects from head and neck (earrings, hairpins, dentures). No fasting required.
entries.positioningInstructions: Patient supine with head in head holder. Orbitomeatal line perpendicular to table.

entries.preparationInstructions: Check renal function (serum creatinine). NPO for 4 hours. Check for contrast allergy history. Ensure IV access.
entries.positioningInstructions: Patient supine with head in head holder. IV contrast injection via power injector.
entries.facilityRequirements: CT scan suite with emergency resuscitation equipment
entries.equipmentRequirements: Multi-detector CT scanner, power injector, crash cart

entries.studyName: CT Abdomen and Pelvis with Contrast
entries.preparationInstructions: NPO for 4-6 hours. Drink oral contrast 1-2 hours before scan. Check renal function. Check contrast allergy history.
entries.positioningInstructions: Patient supine with arms above head. Scan from dome of diaphragm to pubic symphysis.
entries.facilityRequirements: CT scan suite with emergency resuscitation equipment
entries.equipmentRequirements: Multi-detector CT scanner (minimum 16-slice), power injector, crash cart

entries.preparationInstructions: Remove metallic objects from chest area. No fasting required. Breathing instructions will be given during scan.
entries.positioningInstructions: Patient supine with arms above head. Thin-section (1-1.25mm) axial images. Prone images may be obtained to differentiate dependent opacity.
entries.equipmentRequirements: Multi-detector CT scanner with HRCT protocol capability

entries.preparationInstructions: Remove all metallic objects. Complete MRI safety screening questionnaire. No fasting required.
entries.positioningInstructions: Patient supine with head in head coil. Sequences include T1W, T2W, FLAIR, DWI, and SWI as needed.
entries.equipmentRequirements: 1.5T or 3.0T MRI scanner with head coil

entries.preparationInstructions: Remove all metallic objects. Complete MRI safety questionnaire. Check renal function (eGFR). Ensure IV access.
entries.positioningInstructions: Patient supine with head in head coil. Pre and post contrast T1W sequences in multiple planes.
contraindications: Severe renal impairment (risk of nephrogenic systemic fibrosis)
entries.facilityRequirements: MRI suite with RF shielding and emergency equipment
entries.equipmentRequirements: 1.5T or 3.0T MRI scanner with head coil, MRI-compatible power injector

entries.preparationInstructions: Remove all metallic objects. Complete MRI safety screening questionnaire. No fasting required.
entries.positioningInstructions: Patient supine with knees slightly flexed on bolster for comfort. Spine coil centered at L3 level.
entries.equipmentRequirements: 1.5T or 3.0T MRI scanner with spine coil

entries.preparationInstructions: Remove all metallic objects. Complete MRI safety screening questionnaire. No fasting required.
entries.positioningInstructions: Patient supine with knee in dedicated knee coil. Slight external rotation (15 degrees) for optimal ACL visualization.
entries.equipmentRequirements: 1.5T or 3.0T MRI scanner with dedicated knee coil

entries.preparationInstructions: Fast for 6-8 hours for optimal gallbladder visualization. Drink 4-5 glasses of water 1 hour before exam (full bladder for pelvic views).
entries.positioningInstructions: Patient supine. May require left/right lateral decubitus positions. Includes liver, gallbladder, pancreas, spleen, kidneys, and aorta.
entries.equipmentRequirements: Ultrasound machine with curvilinear (3.5-5 MHz) and linear (7-12 MHz) transducers

entries.preparationInstructions: Drink 4-5 glasses of water 1 hour before exam. Full bladder required for transabdominal approach.
entries.positioningInstructions: Patient supine for transabdominal scan. May require transvaginal approach (empty bladder) for better visualization.
entries.equipmentRequirements: Ultrasound machine with curvilinear (3.5-5 MHz) and endocavitary (5-9 MHz) transducers

entries.preparationInstructions: No special preparation required. Remove necklaces and neck jewelry.
entries.positioningInstructions: Patient supine with neck slightly hyperextended using a pillow under shoulders. High-frequency linear transducer over anterior neck.
entries.equipmentRequirements: Ultrasound machine with high-frequency linear transducer (7-15 MHz)

entries.preparationInstructions: No special preparation required. Remove necklaces and jewelry from neck area.
entries.positioningInstructions: Patient supine with head turned slightly to contralateral side. Bilateral common, internal, and external carotid arteries evaluated with B-mode and Doppler.
entries.facilityRequirements: Vascular ultrasound lab or radiology room
entries.equipmentRequirements: Ultrasound machine with linear transducer (7-12 MHz) and color/pulsed-wave Doppler capability

entries.preparationInstructions: No special preparation required. Wear loose comfortable clothing. ECG leads will be attached.
entries.positioningInstructions: Patient in left lateral decubitus position. Standard views: parasternal long axis, parasternal short axis, apical 4-chamber, apical 2-chamber, subcostal.
entries.equipmentRequirements: Echocardiography machine with cardiac phased-array transducer (2-4 MHz), M-mode, 2D, color Doppler, and tissue Doppler capability

entries.preparationInstructions: Drink 4-5 glasses of water 1 hour before exam. Full bladder required for bladder evaluation. No fasting required.
entries.positioningInstructions: Patient supine and lateral decubitus for kidney views. Supine with full bladder for bladder evaluation. Includes measurement of post-void residual if needed.
entries.equipmentRequirements: Ultrasound machine with curvilinear transducer (3.5-5 MHz)
